text: Hello, I am Dr. Richard Fortunato from Allegheny General Hospital in Pittsburgh. And today I'd like to show you a problem with the double stapled technique to be aware of and to avoid. It's the colon rolling away. And here you can see I'm doing the stapling approach for my common

The surgeon elevates the colon, demonstrating how it rolls away at an angle, which can lead to a non-perpendicular staple firing. The surgeon readjusts the stapler and elevates the bowel to achieve a perpendicular staple line with good perfusion for the planned anastomosis.

The surgeon checks the alignment of the colon to ensure it is positioned perpendicular for stapling. The stapler is fired successfully, creating a flat staple line across the bowel. A second firing is performed to maintain a perpendicular staple line, preventing any curvature that could narrow the lumen.
